column2: How do you protect FSLogix profiles against data corruption, datacenter failures, and regional outages?
column3: We use zone-redundant storage to replicate data synchronously across Azure availability zones.

column2: How do you protect FSLogix profiles against data corruption, datacenter failures, and regional outages?
column3: We use the FSLogix cloud cache feature to replicate profiles across regions.

column2: How do you protect FSLogix profiles against data corruption, datacenter failures, and regional outages?
column3: None of the above

column2: How do you protect your virtual network against datacenter and Azure region failures?
column3: We set up a virtual network in our secondary region for failover.

column2: How do you protect your virtual network against datacenter and Azure region failures?
column3: We use Azure Site Recovery to set up a virtual network in our failover region.

column2: How do you protect your virtual network against datacenter and Azure region failures?
column3: None of the above

column2: How do you protect your golden images against data corruption, datacenter failures, and regional outages?
column3: We use Azure Compute Gallery to store images.

column2: How do you protect your golden images against data corruption, datacenter failures, and regional outages?
column3: We use zone-redundant storage to spread copies of images across availability zones.

column2: How do you protect your golden images against data corruption, datacenter failures, and regional outages?
column3: We create a secondary compute gallery within a secondary region.

column2: How do you protect your golden images against data corruption, datacenter failures, and regional outages?
column3: None of the above

column2: What factors do you consider when you select your deployment regions for Azure Virtual Desktop?
column3: We check that the virtual machine (VM) SKUs and Azure services that we need are available in our selected region.

column2: What factors do you consider when you select your deployment regions for Azure Virtual Desktop?
column3: Before we select a region, we familiarize ourselves with its compliance and data residency requirements.

column2: What factors do you consider when you select your deployment regions for Azure Virtual Desktop?
column3: To minimize latency and reduce data transfer costs, we place our resources in the same Azure region as our host pool.

column2: What factors do you consider when you select your deployment regions for Azure Virtual Desktop?
column3: We familiarize ourselves with the regions that Azure Virtual Desktop management plane resources are available in.

column2: What factors do you consider when you select your deployment regions for Azure Virtual Desktop?
column3: None of the above

column2: What factors do you consider when you select a virtual machine (VM) SKU and a disk type?
column3: Before we select a VM SKU, we examine the CPU, GPU, memory, and storage usage of the workloads that our users run.

column2: What factors do you consider when you select a virtual machine (VM) SKU and a disk type?
column3: For single-session hosts, we use VMs with at least two physical CPU cores.

column2: What factors do you consider when you select a virtual machine (VM) SKU and a disk type?
column3: For multiple-session hosts, we use VMs with at least four cores.

column2: What factors do you consider when you select a virtual machine (VM) SKU and a disk type?
column3: We use a larger number of smaller VMs instead of a few large VMs.

column2: What factors do you consider when you select a virtual machine (VM) SKU and a disk type?
column3: We use scaling plans to adjust our number of VMs based on user demand and schedule.

column2: What factors do you consider when you select a virtual machine (VM) SKU and a disk type?
column3: When we select a disk type, we take into account the I/O operations per second (IOPS) and throughput performance limits of our VMs.

column2: What factors do you consider when you select a virtual machine (VM) SKU and a disk type?
column3: For high-performance workloads, we use solid-state drives (SSDs).

column2: What factors do you consider when you select a virtual machine (VM) SKU and a disk type?
column3: For production workloads that require high performance, low latency, and a service-level agreement (SLA), we use premium SSDs.

column2: What factors do you consider when you select a virtual machine (VM) SKU and a disk type?
column3: For Windows 10 or Windows 11 Enterprise Multi-Session, we use premium SSDs.

column2: What factors do you consider when you select a virtual machine (VM) SKU and a disk type?
column3: For personal desktops, we use standard or premium SSDs.

column2: What factors do you consider when you select a virtual machine (VM) SKU and a disk type?
column3: None of the above

column2: If you use FSLogix as a user profile solution, which performance, capacity, and infrastructure requirements do you consider before you select an FSLogix storage solution?
column3: We deploy our storage solution for FSLogix profile containers in the same region as our session hosts.

column2: If you use FSLogix as a user profile solution, which performance, capacity, and infrastructure requirements do you consider before you select an FSLogix storage solution?
column3: Before we select a storage solution, we check that it supports the protocols that we use.

column2: If you use FSLogix as a user profile solution, which performance, capacity, and infrastructure requirements do you consider before you select an FSLogix storage solution?
column3: In most scenarios, we use Azure Files as our storage solution for FSLogix.

column2: If you use FSLogix as a user profile solution, which performance, capacity, and infrastructure requirements do you consider before you select an FSLogix storage solution?
column3: For I/O-intensive workloads that require high performance and low latency, we use Azure Files premium file shares.

column2: If you use FSLogix as a user profile solution, which performance, capacity, and infrastructure requirements do you consider before you select an FSLogix storage solution?
column3: For I/O workloads that are less sensitive to performance variability, we use Azure Files standard file shares.

column2: If you use FSLogix as a user profile solution, which performance, capacity, and infrastructure requirements do you consider before you select an FSLogix storage solution?
column3: For a pay-as-you-go billing model, we use Azure Files standard file shares.

column2: If you use FSLogix as a user profile solution, which performance, capacity, and infrastructure requirements do you consider before you select an FSLogix storage solution?
column3: For large-scale Azure Virtual Desktop deployments, we use Azure NetApp Files as a storage solution for FSLogix.

column2: If you use FSLogix as a user profile solution, which performance, capacity, and infrastructure requirements do you consider before you select an FSLogix storage solution?
column3: We check that Azure NetApp Files is available in our region.

column2: If you use FSLogix as a user profile solution, which performance, capacity, and infrastructure requirements do you consider before you select an FSLogix storage solution?
column3: We consider performance requirements and costs when we select a tier and provision capacity for Azure NetApp Files.

column2: If you use FSLogix as a user profile solution, which performance, capacity, and infrastructure requirements do you consider before you select an FSLogix storage solution?
column3: We're aware that the capacity that we provision for Azure NetApp Files can affect our choice of tier.

column2: If you use FSLogix as a user profile solution, which performance, capacity, and infrastructure requirements do you consider before you select an FSLogix storage solution?
column3: None of the above

column2: Do you use App Attach to deliver applications to Azure Virtual Desktop virtual machines (VMs)?
column3: We use App Attach, and we separate its storage fabric from FSLogix profile containers.

column2: Do you use App Attach to deliver applications to Azure Virtual Desktop virtual machines (VMs)?
column3: We use App Attach, and to avoid performance bottlenecks, we exclude certain files and locations from antivirus scans.

column2: Do you use App Attach to deliver applications to Azure Virtual Desktop virtual machines (VMs)?
column3: We use App Attach with a multiple-session host, and we ran a test to see whether two or more users on the same session host can successfully run the app at the same time.

column2: Do you use App Attach to deliver applications to Azure Virtual Desktop virtual machines (VMs)?
column3: In our disaster recovery plans for Azure Virtual Desktop, we include steps for replicating our App Attach file share in our secondary failover location, and we ensure that the file share path is accessible in the secondary location.

column2: Do you use App Attach to deliver applications to Azure Virtual Desktop virtual machines (VMs)?
column3: We use Azure Files, and we checked that the App Attach file share contains the Storage File Data SMB Share Reader and Storage File Data SMB Share Elevated Contributor permissions.

column2: Do you use App Attach to deliver applications to Azure Virtual Desktop virtual machines (VMs)?
column3: None of the above
